description:
You are the platform engineer on-call.  
    The nightly build for 2024-05-01 has produced a raw error log that you need to summarise for the SRE dashboard.

    1. Source data  
       • Location: /home/user/ci_pipeline/logs/build_2024-05-01.log  
       • The file already exists and is readable by your normal user.  
       • Every line is guaranteed to follow **exactly** this pattern  
         “[ISO-8601-timestamp] ERROR <CODE>: <free-text message>”  
         e.g.  
         “[2024-05-01T12:00:01Z] ERROR E101: Dependency download failed”

    2. Required output  
       • Create the directory /home/user/ci_pipeline/reports if it does not already exist.  
       • Generate a frequency table of the error codes using only standard GNU utilities available in a minimal Linux shell (e.g. sort, uniq, cut, awk, etc.).  
       • Write the final table to the file  
         /home/user/ci_pipeline/reports/error_code_summary_2024-05-01.txt  
       • The file **must** conform to the exact format below (this is what the automated checker will read).  
         ‑ The very first line is a header:  
           ERROR_CODE<TAB>COUNT  
           (i.e. literal text “ERROR_CODE”, one horizontal tab, then “COUNT”).  
         ‑ Each subsequent line contains one distinct error code followed by its integer occurrence count, separated by a single horizontal tab character.  
         ‑ Lines must be sorted by COUNT in strictly descending numeric order.  
         ‑ If two codes have the same count, their relative order must be ASCII ascending by the error code text.  
         ‑ No extra spaces, no quotation marks, no trailing whitespace.  
         Example of the **expected shape** (the numbers here are illustrative only):  
           ERROR_CODE<TAB>COUNT  
           E202<TAB>4  
           E101<TAB>3  
           E303<TAB>1  
           E404<TAB>1

    3. Verification log (for human peer review)  
       • After creating the summary file, append a one-liner to /home/user/ci_pipeline/reports/notes.log in the form:  
         “generated error_code_summary_2024-05-01.txt at YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS”  
         where the timestamp is the current system local time.  
       • The automated test will not check notes.log, but real teammates will, so make sure the line is there.

    4. What the automated grader will test  
       a) That /home/user/ci_pipeline/reports/error_code_summary_2024-05-01.txt exists.  
       b) That its first line is exactly “ERROR_CODE<TAB>COUNT”.  
       c) That each subsequent line contains “E###<TAB><number>”.  
       d) That the numeric counts are correct for the supplied log file.  
       e) That the lines are in the required order.  

    Only the text file described above will be graded; your terminal output is ignored by the checker, but feel free to print progress to the console for your own debugging.
truth:
<!-- Pre-existing artefacts -->
    <preexistingFile path="/home/user/ci_pipeline/logs/build_2024-05-01.log"><![CDATA[
[2024-05-01T12:00:01Z] ERROR E101: Dependency download failed
[2024-05-01T12:00:02Z] ERROR E202: Unit test failure
[2024-05-01T12:00:03Z] ERROR E101: Dependency download failed
[2024-05-01T12:00:04Z] ERROR E303: Linting error
[2024-05-01T12:00:05Z] ERROR E101: Dependency download failed
[2024-05-01T12:00:06Z] ERROR E202: Unit test failure
[2024-05-01T12:00:07Z] ERROR E404: Resource not found
[2024-05-01T12:00:08Z] ERROR E202: Unit test failure
[2024-05-01T12:00:09Z] ERROR E202: Unit test failure
]]></preexistingFile>

    <!-- Expected artefacts after successful task completion -->
    <expectedFile path="/home/user/ci_pipeline/reports/error_code_summary_2024-05-01.txt"><![CDATA[
ERROR_CODE	COUNT
E202	4
E101	3
E303	1
E404	1
]]></expectedFile>

    <!-- The checker will also confirm that the directory /home/user/ci_pipeline/reports exists.
         notes.log is not strictly graded but may be present. -->
